heading: International outreach program
label: 2329 International outreach program
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1992, and not as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 which comprises this chapter.

Amendments2016—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) authorized the Secretary to engage in activities to inform the United States maritime industry and port authorities of technological innovations abroad that could significantly improve waterborne transportation in the United States, both inland and deep draft.Pub. L. 114–322
“Secretary” DefinedSecretary means the Secretary of the Army, see , set out as a note under .section 3 of Pub. L. 102–580section 2201 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may engage in activities to inform the United States of technological innovations abroad that could significantly improve water resources development in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: development, monitoring, assessment, and dissemination of information about foreign water resources projects that could significantly improve water resources development in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: research, development, training, and other forms of technology transfer and exchange; and

children.label: C
children.text: offering technical services that cannot be readily obtained in the private sector to be incorporated into water resources projects if the costs for assistance will be recovered under the terms of each project.

children.heading: Cooperation
children.label: b Cooperation
children.text: The Secretary may carry out the provisions of this section in cooperation with Federal departments and agencies, State and local agencies, authorities, institutions, corporations (profit or nonprofit), foreign governments, or other organizations.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: c Funding
children.text: The funds to carry out the provisions of this section shall include funds deposited in a special account with the Secretary of the Treasury for such purposes by any cooperating entity or organization according to cost-sharing agreements proscribed by the Secretary. Reimbursement for services provided under this section shall be credited to the appropriation concerned.
